title: deutzia scabra var. microcarpa
intro: Deutzia scabra var. microcarpa is a deciduous shrub native to Japan, belonging to the Hydrangeaceae family. It is also known as the dwarf Deutzia or small-flowered Deutzia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, where its roots and stems are valued for their medicinal properties, particularly as a treatment for fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the roots and stems of Deutzia scabra var. microcarpa are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that are believed to promote health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving circulation.
